Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Vacated

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-18-276755, CGC-19-276777, CGC-19-276786, CGC-19-276793, CGC-19-276798, CGC-20-276818, CGC-20-276820 WITH CASE CGC-18-276734
Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs.
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.
Shelly K. Tinkoff of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant Lamons Gasket Company.
Emily Anselmo of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant General Electric Company and Viacom CBS Inc.
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Foster Wheeler.
Thomas Steig of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendant Consolidated Insulation, Inc.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling amended as follows: Plaintiffs' Motion to Consolidate three Personal Injury/Brayton/Preference Cases or, In the Alternative, Motion for Joinder of Cases Pursuant to C.C.P. 378, and to Proceed with Trial by Alternative Means is DENIED. Court granted Plaintiff request for withdrawal for Harvey Dwain Olsen vs Metalclad Insulation, LLC. (CGC-20-276820) from Motion. Per Plaintiffs, Reid et al. v. Asbestos Corporation Limited et al. (276734) and Rideaux et al. v. General Electric Company et al. (276777) have been withdrawn from the motion. No opposition filed. Plaintiffs fail to sufficiently show a common question of law or fact as required under C.C.P. 1048 and 348. Plaintiffs fail to satisfy the Malcolm factors of common worksite, similar occupation, similar time of exposure, and type of disease. (Malcolm v. Nat'l Gypsum Co. (1993) 995 F.2d 346, 350-51.) Regarding Plaintiffs' request for the Court order the trials to proceed "by alternative means" and that the unified trials proceed on the earliest dates, the trial judge has discretion to determine the manner and procedures to be used at trial, as consistent with health and safety directives of the state and county authority. Each case will proceed on the trial dates set in the case sought to be consolidated, as ordered by the Court when preference in that case was granted. Court to issue and serve order. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-589-3651) csrkim@comcast.net (503/CML)

RULING: PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Defendant CIL&D, LLC filed a joinder to oppositions by Defendants Sunoco, Inc. (R&M) and Sun Ship, LLC's, which were withdrawn on June 3, 2020. The trial date is September 28, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 7, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 24, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 11, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 25, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 3, 2020. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Jun-09-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than June 18, 2020 plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than June 18, 2020 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on August 5, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than August 14, 2020. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 28, 2020. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by September 18, 2020 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, September 24, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
